title: Share of cereals allocated to industrial uses
subtitle: Share of cereals available for domestic consumption – after accounting for trade – that is allocated to other purposes (primarily industrial uses such as biofuel production), relative to the total allocated to human food, animal feed, and other uses. This excludes cereals used for seed, processing, and losses.
caption:
Share of cereals allocated to industrial uses

Share of cereals available for domestic consumption – after accounting for trade – that is allocated to other purposes (primarily industrial uses such as biofuel production), relative to the total allocated to human food, animal feed, and other uses. This excludes cereals used for seed, processing, and losses.

This choropleth world map displays the share of cereals allocated to industrial uses in 2023. The legend ranges from 0% to over 20%, with specific tick marks at 0%, 1%, 2%, 5%, 10%, and 20%. The darkest green band, representing shares above 20%, is occupied by the United States and Canada in North America, as well as Thailand in Southeast Asia. A mid-range band, covering values between 5% and 10%, includes countries such as Australia, Argentina, Brazil, China, and Russia. Much of Western Europe, including France and Germany, along with Mexico and India, appears in lighter green shades that correspond to the 2% to 5% range. Large portions of Africa, the Middle East, and parts of Asia are colored in the lightest shades, indicating shares near or below 1%. Several regions, including Greenland, parts of Central Africa, and Yemen, are marked with diagonal hatching to indicate no data is available. The visual distribution highlights a concentration of higher industrial allocation in North America and specific agricultural exporters in Asia and South America, while many developing nations show minimal allocation to this sector.
